text:
There was a bird who couldn’t stop singing. Every morning it chirped so delightfully. Nearby, an old oak tree listened quietly. One day, while listening, the tree had an epiphany. “Why sing?” The oak thought. “Why not share wisdom? I’ll start a sermon.”

Each evening, the tree would pour out a speech about things ancient, profound, and utterly pointless:

“First, the clouds dance in patterns. Why? Because it soothes their spirits. Why? Because they live for that. Secondly, moths love honey. Why? Because sugar tastes nice. Why? Because insects can’t taste. Finally, some say love is blind. Why? Because if we knew someone was ugly, we’d avoid them. Do you really think that makes sense?”

The birds, attentive to the old tree, listened. Some learned from its reflections. Others scoffed. Yet, some embraced what the oak had to say.
